addedBlocks:
## lc
LemonCrow replaces the grep→read→re-read loop.
- Lead with `code_search`: one call = ranked matches (top source inline, the rest as precise path:Lx-Ly pointers) + related_symbols + candidate_files. Inline source = already read. Never shell-grep workspace code or re-verify indexed results.
- Known path/symbols →  batch `read`: ONE call, files=[...], exact :Lx-Ly ranges, never the same file twice. Never cat/sed/head/tail.
- ALL edits in ONE `edit` edits[] array; prefer {path: 'f.py:Lx-Ly', new} over old/new.
- `bash` = execution only (tests, git, builds).
- Large output → a file, never inline prose.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: wc -l /app/text.gcode; head -50 /app/text.gcode
grep -n "M486" /app/text.gcode | head -40; echo ---; grep -c "M486 S0" /app/text.gcode; grep -c "M486 S1" /app/text.gcode

cat > /tmp/parse.py <<'EOF'
import re
segs=[]  # (x0,y0,x1,y1)
x=y=e=0.0
absE=False
cur=None
with open('/app/text.gcode') as f:
    for line in f:
        s=line.strip()
        if s.startswith('M486'):
            m=re.match(r'M486 S(-?\d+)',s)
            if m: cur=int(m.group(1))
            continue
        if s.startswith('M82'): absE=True; continue
        if s.startswith('M83'): absE=False; continue
        if s.startswith('G92'):
            m=re.search(r'E(-?[\d.]+)',s)
            if m: e=float(m.group(1))
            continue
        if s.startswith('G1') or s.startswith('G0'):
            nx,ny=x,y; de=0.0; hasE=False
            for tok in s.split():
                if tok[0]=='X': nx=float(tok[1:])
                elif tok[0]=='Y': ny=float(tok[1:])
                elif tok[0]=='E':
                    hasE=True
                    val=float(tok[1:])
                    de=(val-e) if absE else val
                    e=val if absE else e
            if hasE and de>0 and (nx!=x or ny!=y) and cur==0:
                segs.append((x,y,nx,ny))
            x,y=nx,ny
print("segs",len(segs))
xs=[p for s in segs for p in (s[0],s[2])]
ys=[p for s in segs for p in (s[1],s[3])]
print("x",min(xs),max(xs),"y",min(ys),max(ys))
import pickle
pickle.dump(segs,open('/tmp/segs.pkl','wb'))
EOF
python3 /tmp/parse.py

cat > /tmp/render.py <<'EOF'
import pickle
segs=pickle.load(open('/tmp/segs.pkl','rb'))
xs=[p for s in segs for p in (s[0],s[2])]
ys=[p for s in segs for p in (s[1],s[3])]
minx,maxx=min(xs),max(xs); miny,maxy=min(ys),max(ys)
W=300
H=int(W*(maxy-miny)/(maxx-minx))
grid=[[' ']*W for _ in range(H)]
def px(x,y):
    cx=int((x-minx)/(maxx-minx)*(W-1))
    cy=int((maxy-y)/(maxy-miny)*(H-1))
    return cx,cy
for x0,y0,x1,y1 in segs:
    n=int(max(abs(x1-x0),abs(y1-y0))*4)+1
    for i in range(n+1):
        t=i/n
        cx,cy=px(x0+(x1-x0)*t,y0+(y1-y0)*t)
        if 0<=cy<H and 0<=cx<W: grid[cy][cx]='#'
with open('/tmp/art.txt','w') as f:
    for row in grid: f.write(''.join(row).rstrip()+'\n')
print(H,W)
EOF
python3 /tmp/render.py
head -60 /tmp/art.txt

cat > /tmp/render2.py <<'EOF'
import pickle
segs=pickle.load(open('/tmp/segs.pkl','rb'))
xs=[p for s in segs for p in (s[0],s[2])]
ys=[p for s in segs for p in (s[1],s[3])]
minx,maxx=min(xs),max(xs); miny,maxy=min(ys),max(ys)
W=150
aspect=(maxy-miny)/(maxx-minx)
H=int(W*aspect*0.5)  # char cell ~2:1
grid=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
def px(x,y):
    cx=int((x-minx)/(maxx-minx)*(W-1))
    cy=int((maxy-y)/(maxy-miny)*(H-1))
    return cx,cy
for x0,y0,x1,y1 in segs:
    n=int(max(abs(x1-x0),abs(y1-y0))*4)+1
    for i in range(n+1):
        t=i/n
        cx,cy=px(x0+(x1-x0)*t,y0+(y1-y0)*t)
        if 0<=cy<H and 0<=cx<W: grid[cy][cx]+=1
with open('/tmp/art2.txt','w') as f:
    for row in grid:
        f.write(''.join('#' if c else ' ' for c in row).rstrip()+'\n')
print(H,W)
EOF
python3 /tmp/render2.py
cat /tmp/art2.txt

grep -n ";TYPE:" /app/text.gcode | sed 's/.*;TYPE:/;TYPE:/' | sort | uniq -c; echo ---; grep -n "^;" /app/text.gcode | grep -iE "height|width|Z:" | head; echo ---HEIGHT---; grep -nE "^;(HEIGHT|Z):" /app/text.gcode | head
grep -n "^;" /app/text.gcode | head -40

cat > /tmp/proj.py <<'EOF'
import re,pickle
# recollect with z
x=y=z=e=0.0; absE=False; cur=None
segs=[]
with open('/app/text.gcode') as f:
    for line in f:
        s=line.strip()
        if s.startswith('M486'):
            m=re.match(r'M486 S(-?\d+)',s)
            if m: cur=int(m.group(1))
            continue
        if s.startswith('M82'):absE=True;continue
        if s.startswith('M83'):absE=False;continue
        if s.startswith('G92'):
            m=re.search(r'E(-?[\d.]+)',s)
            if m:e=float(m.group(1))
            continue
        if s[:2] in ('G1','G0'):
            nx,ny,nz=x,y,z;de=0;hasE=False
            for tok in s.split():
                if not tok:continue
                c=tok[0]
                try:v=float(tok[1:])
                except:continue
                if c=='X':nx=v
                elif c=='Y':ny=v
                elif c=='Z':nz=v
                elif c=='E':
                    hasE=True;de=(v-e) if absE else v
                    if absE:e=v
            if hasE and de>0 and (nx!=x or ny!=y or nz!=z) and cur==0:
                segs.append((x,y,z,nx,ny,nz))
            x,y,z=nx,ny,nz
pickle.dump(segs,open('/tmp/seg3.pkl','wb'))
def render(ai,bi,W,flipb=True,name=''):
    a=[c for s in segs for c in (s[ai],s[ai+3])]
    b=[c for s in segs for c in (s[bi],s[bi+3])]
    mina,maxa=min(a),max(a);minb,maxb=min(b),max(b)
    aspect=(maxb-minb)/(maxa-mina)
    H=max(3,int(W*aspect*0.5))
    g=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
    for s in segs:
        x0,y0=s[ai],s[bi];x1,y1=s[ai+3],s[bi+3]
        n=int(max(abs(x1-x0),abs(y1-y0))*4)+1
        for i in range(n+1):
            t=i/n;xa=x0+(x1-x0)*t;ba=y0+(y1-y0)*t
            cx=int((xa-mina)/(maxa-mina)*(W-1))
            cy=int((maxb-ba)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1)) if flipb else int((ba-minb)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1))
            if 0<=cy<H and 0<=cx<W:g[cy][cx]+=1
    print("==",name,"a",mina,maxa,"b",minb,maxb)
    for row in g:print(''.join('#' if c else ' ' for c in row).rstrip())
render(0,2,150,name='XZ')  # x vs z
EOF
python3 /tmp/proj.py

input.command:
uv pip install --system --break-system-packages numpy -q 2>/dev/null; cat > /tmp/pca.py <<'EOF'
import pickle,numpy as np
segs=pickle.load(open('/tmp/seg3.pkl','rb'))
P=[]
for s in segs:
    P.append(s[0:3]);P.append(s[3:6])
P=np.array(P)
c=P.mean(0)
U,S,Vt=np.linalg.svd(P-c)
print("singular",S[:3] if len(S)>=3 else S)
print("axes",Vt)
# project seg endpoints onto top2 components
comp=Vt[:2]
def render(u_idx=0,v_idx=1,W=200,flip=True):
    A=[]
    for s in segs:
        p0=np.array(s[0:3])-c;p1=np.array(s[3:6])-c
        a0=p0@Vt[u_idx];b0=p0@Vt[v_idx]
        a1=p1@Vt[u_idx];b1=p1@Vt[v_idx]
        A.append((a0,b0,a1,b1))
    aa=[x for t in A for x in (t[0],t[2])]
    bb=[x for t in A for x in (t[1],t[3])]
    mina,maxa=min(aa),max(aa);minb,maxb=min(bb),max(bb)
    aspect=(maxb-minb)/(maxa-mina)
    H=max(3,int(W*aspect*0.5))
    g=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
    for a0,b0,a1,b1 in A:
        n=int(max(abs(a1-a0),abs(b1-b0))*4)+1
        for i in range(n+1):
            t=i/n;xa=a0+(a1-a0)*t;ba=b0+(b1-b0)*t
            cx=int((xa-mina)/(maxa-mina)*(W-1))
            cy=int((maxb-ba)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1)) if flip else int((ba-minb)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1))
            g[cy][cx]+=1
    print("H",H,"aspect",round(aspect,3))
    for row in g:print(''.join('#' if v else ' ' for v in row).rstrip())
render()
EOF
python3 /tmp/pca.py

cat >> /tmp/pca.py <<'EOF'

def render_file(W=1400,flip=True,fx=1.0,fy=1.0,path='/tmp/pcatext.txt'):
    A=[]
    for s in segs:
        p0=np.array(s[0:3])-c;p1=np.array(s[3:6])-c
        A.append((p0@Vt[0],p0@Vt[1],p1@Vt[0],p1@Vt[1]))
    aa=[x for t in A for x in (t[0],t[2])];bb=[x for t in A for x in (t[1],t[3])]
    mina,maxa=min(aa),max(aa);minb,maxb=min(bb),max(bb)
    aspect=(maxb-minb)/(maxa-mina)
    H=max(3,int(W*aspect))
    g=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
    for a0,b0,a1,b1 in A:
        n=int(max(abs(a1-a0),abs(b1-b0))*6)+1
        for i in range(n+1):
            t=i/n;xa=a0+(a1-a0)*t;ba=b0+(b1-b0)*t
            cx=int((xa-mina)/(maxa-mina)*(W-1)) if fx>0 else int((maxa-xa)/(maxa-mina)*(W-1))
            cy=int((maxb-ba)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1)) if flip else int((ba-minb)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1))
            g[cy][cx]+=1
    with open(path,'w') as f:
        for row in g:f.write(''.join('#' if v else ' ' for v in row).rstrip()+'\n')
    print("wrote",path,"H",H,"W",W)
render_file()
EOF
python3 /tmp/pca.py 2>&1 | tail -2

python3 - <<'EOF'
lines=open('/tmp/pcatext.txt').read().split('\n')
# crop empty rows
rows=[l for l in lines]
# find nonempty col range per band, print bands of width 230, downsample rows by 3
import sys
maxw=max(len(l) for l in rows)
rows=[l.ljust(maxw) for l in rows]
# downsample vertically factor 3
ds=[]
for i in range(0,len(rows),3):
    block=rows[i:i+3]
    merged=''.join('#' if any(b[j]=='#' for b in block) else ' ' for j in range(maxw))
    ds.append(merged)
band=240
for start in range(0,maxw,band):
    print("==== cols",start,start+band)
    for r in ds:
        print(r[start:start+band].rstrip())
    print()
EOF

cat >> /tmp/pca.py <<'EOF'

def r2(W=560,fac=0.5,path='/tmp/t2.txt',mirror=False):
    A=[]
    for s in segs:
        p0=np.array(s[0:3])-c;p1=np.array(s[3:6])-c
        A.append((p0@Vt[0],p0@Vt[1],p1@Vt[0],p1@Vt[1]))
    aa=[x for t in A for x in (t[0],t[2])];bb=[x for t in A for x in (t[1],t[3])]
    mina,maxa=min(aa),max(aa);minb,maxb=min(bb),max(bb)
    aspect=(maxb-minb)/(maxa-mina)
    H=max(3,int(W*aspect*fac))
    g=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
    for a0,b0,a1,b1 in A:
        n=int(max(abs(a1-a0),abs(b1-b0))*6)+1
        for i in range(n+1):
            t=i/n;xa=a0+(a1-a0)*t;ba=b0+(b1-b0)*t
            if mirror: cx=int((maxa-xa)/(maxa-mina)*(W-1))
            else: cx=int((xa-mina)/(maxa-mina)*(W-1))
            cy=int((maxb-ba)/(maxb-minb)*(H-1))
            g[cy][cx]+=1
    with open(path,'w') as f:
        for row in g:f.write(''.join('#' if v else ' ' for v in row).rstrip()+'\n')
    print("wrote",path,H,W)
r2(mirror=False,path='/tmp/t_norm.txt')
r2(mirror=True,path='/tmp/t_mir.txt')
EOF
python3 /tmp/pca.py 2>&1|tail -2

input.command:
cat > /tmp/img.py <<'EOF'
import pickle,numpy as np
from PIL import Image,ImageDraw
segs=pickle.load(open('/tmp/seg3.pkl','rb'))
P=np.array([p for s in segs for p in (s[0:3],s[3:6])])
c=P.mean(0)
U,S,Vt=np.linalg.svd(P-c,full_matrices=False)
A=[]
for s in segs:
    p0=np.array(s[0:3])-c;p1=np.array(s[3:6])-c
    A.append((p0@Vt[0],p0@Vt[1],p1@Vt[0],p1@Vt[1]))
aa=[x for t in A for x in (t[0],t[2])];bb=[x for t in A for x in (t[1],t[3])]
mina,maxa=min(aa),max(aa);minb,maxb=min(bb),max(bb)
def make(mirror,path,scale=12,pad=20):
    W=int((maxa-mina)*scale)+2*pad
    H=int((maxb-minb)*scale)+2*pad
    im=Image.new('L',(W,H),0);d=ImageDraw.Draw(im)
    for a0,b0,a1,b1 in A:
        if mirror:
            x0=(maxa-a0)*scale+pad;x1=(maxa-a1)*scale+pad
        else:
            x0=(a0-mina)*scale+pad;x1=(a1-mina)*scale+pad
        y0=(maxb-b0)*scale+pad;y1=(maxb-b1)*scale+pad
        d.line([(x0,y0),(x1,y1)],fill=255,width=max(1,int(scale*0.45)))
    im.save(path);print(path,W,H)
make(False,'/tmp/norm.png')
make(True,'/tmp/mir.png')
EOF
python3 /tmp/img.py
echo "--- OCR normal ---"; tesseract /tmp/norm.png stdout --psm 7 2>/dev/null
echo "--- OCR mirror ---"; tesseract /tmp/mir.png stdout --psm 7 2>/dev/null

from PIL import Image
im=Image.open('/tmp/norm.png').convert('L')
W,H=im.size
# region roughly cols for "gc0d3_iz" : after flag{ . Estimate flag{ occupies ~ first 6 chars of 26 -> fraction
# 26 chars total width. char idx: f0 l1 a2 g3 {4 g5 c6 0/O7 d8 3/e9 _10 i11 z12
import numpy as np
arr=np.array(im)
# print ascii of full but downsample to width 200
def show(a,b,cols=170):
    sub=arr[:,a:b]
    h,w=sub.shape
    rows=40
    out=[]
    for r in range(rows):
        y0=int(r*h/rows);y1=int((r+1)*h/rows)
        line=''
        for cc in range(cols):
            x0=int(cc*w/cols);x1=int((cc+1)*w/cols)
            block=sub[y0:y1,x0:x1]
            line+='#' if block.max()>80 else ' '
        out.append(line.rstrip())
    print('\n'.join(out))

u=W/26
show(int(4.6*u),int(13.5*u))

cd /tmp; python3 - <<'EOF'
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np
im=Image.open('/tmp/norm.png').convert('L')
arr=np.array(im)
col=(arr.max(0)>80)
# find runs of ink separated by blanks
runs=[];i=0;W=len(col)
while i<W:
    if col[i]:
        j=i
        while j<W and (col[j] or (j+1<W and col[j+1]) or (j+2<W and col[j+2])):
            j+=1
        runs.append((i,j));i=j
    else:i+=1
print(len(runs),"runs")
for k,(a,b) in enumerate(runs):
    Image.open('/tmp/norm.png').crop((a-4,0,b+4,arr.shape[0])).save(f'/tmp/g{k}.png')
res=[]
import subprocess
for k in range(len(runs)):
    o=subprocess.run(['tesseract',f'/tmp/g{k}.png','stdout','--psm','10'],capture_output=True,text=True).stdout.strip()
    res.append(o)
    print(k,runs[k],repr(o))
EOF

cd /tmp; python3 - <<'EOF'
import pickle,numpy as np
segs=pickle.load(open('/tmp/seg3.pkl','rb'))
P=np.array([p for s in segs for p in (s[0:3],s[3:6])])
c=P.mean(0)
U,S,Vt=np.linalg.svd(P-c,full_matrices=False)
# projected coords per seg endpoint
A=[]
for s in segs:
    p0=np.array(s[0:3])-c;p1=np.array(s[3:6])-c
    A.append((p0@Vt[0],p0@Vt[1],p1@Vt[0],p1@Vt[1]))
A=np.array(A)
aa=np.concatenate([A[:,0],A[:,2]]);bb=np.concatenate([A[:,1],A[:,3]])
mina,maxa=aa.min(),aa.max();minb,maxb=bb.min(),bb.max()
scale=2058/(maxa-mina);pad=0  # match earlier? earlier W scale=12 with pad20. Just map to px like norm.png
# norm.png used scale=12,pad=20
sc=12;pad=20
def X(a): return (a-mina)*sc+pad
def Yb(b): return b  # keep in projected units for height
# runs in px from before:
runs=[(18,58),(69,86),(101,178),(185,260),(265,319),(325,398),(412,482),(490,567),(577,650),(663,739),(749,829),(841,858),(871,1032),(1036,1106),(1118,1188),(1200,1278),(1294,1368),(1380,1454),(1462,1537),(1552,1622),(1638,1742),(1755,1785),(1797,1889),(1904,1977),(1985,2045)]
labels=['f','l','a','g','{','g','c','0','d','3','_','i','z','c','h','4','l','l','e','n','g','i','N','g','}']
# baseline & capline: use 'l'(idx1) ascender top and its bottom, 'a'(2) x-height top
# compute per run top(maxb) bottom(minb)
# map seg endpoints to px x for filtering
segpx=[]
for (a0,b0,a1,b1) in A:
    segpx.append((X(a0),b0,X(a1),b1))
segpx=np.array(segpx)
def rng(a,b):
    m=((segpx[:,0]>=a-2)&(segpx[:,0]<=b+2))|((segpx[:,2]>=a-2)&(segpx[:,2]<=b+2))
    bs=np.concatenate([segpx[m,1],segpx[m,3]])
    return bs.min(),bs.max()
for (a,b),lab in zip(runs,labels):
    lo,hi=rng(a,b)
    print(f"{lab:2} x{a:4}-{b:4} top={hi:7.2f} bot={lo:7.2f} h={hi-lo:5.2f}")
EOF
